DET.note: Determiners (a, the) must be followed by a noun or an adjective describing a noun.

ADP.note: Prepositions (of, in) usually lead to a noun phrase.

ADV.note: Adverbs modify verbs (quickly run), adjectives (so frustrating), or other adverbs.

CCONJ.note: Conjunctions (and, but, or) can join almost any parallel structures.

ADJ.note: Adjectives should almost always stack or end in a noun.

NOUN.note: Nouns are usually followed by an action (verb) or a relationship (preposition).

PRON.note: Pronouns (I, we, it) are almost always followed by a verb.

PART.note: Particles like 'to' or 'not' usually lead directly into a base verb or adverb (to demonstrate, not typing).

SCONJ.note: Subordinating conjunctions (because, if, while) start new clauses, usually followed by a subject.
